steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject is "Anthropologist Lívia Barbosa" — one person, singular. The long prepositional phrase "of the Universidade Federal Fluminense in Brazil" modifies the subject but does not change it. The blank needs a singular present-tense verb.

steps.title: Test each choice against subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'studies': Third-person singular present tense — matches a singular subject. ✓
  • Choice B — 'are studying': Plural verb form — does not agree with a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'have studied': Plural auxiliary — does not agree with a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'study': Base form used for plural subjects or first-person — does not agree with a third-person singular subject. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
